name: Bertha "Chippie" Hill
description: American blues and vaudeville singer and dancer (1905-1950)
abstract: Bertha "Chippie" Hill (March 15, 1905 – May 7, 1950), was an American blues and vaudeville singer and dancer, best known for her recordings with Louis Armstrong.

concerts.label: Kid Ory and His Creole Jazz Band

works.t: Baby, Won't You Please Come Home
How Come You Do Me Like You Do?

about_quotes.speaker: Sippie Wallace
about_quotes.evidence: She sang my "Shorty George Blues." She asked me to let her sing... She was [at that big night].

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: But what a tenor... And they had a girl there named Bertha Hill... She had a sweet voice. Then you had May Alex... none of us used a microphone... like Bertha.
